info.description:
| \ | | | ____| |_   _|   |  _ \  | ____| |  _ \  | |
|  \| | |  _|     | |     | |_) | |  _|   | |_) | | |
_  | |\  | | |___    | |     |  _ <  | |___  |  __/  | |___
(_) |_| \_| |_____|   |_|     |_| \_\ |_____| |_|     |_____|
🔵 Start the REPL like this:
dotnet-repl [options]
┌───────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Option                                │ Description                          │
├───────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ -h, -?, --help                        │ Show help and usage information      │
│ --version                             │ Show version information             │
│ --log-path <PATH>                     │ Enable file logging to the specified │
│                                       │ directory                            │
│ --default-kernel <csharp|fsharp|http| │ The default language for the kernel  │
│ javascript|pwsh>                      │                                      │
│ --run <PATH>                          │ Run all of the code in the specified │
│                                       │ notebook, source code, or script     │
│                                       │ file. To exit when done, set the     │
│                                       │ --exit-after-run option.             │
│ --working-dir <--WORKING-DIR>         │ Working directory to which to change │
│                                       │ after launching the kernel           │
│ --exit-after-run                      │ Exit after the file specified by     │
│                                       │ --run has run                        │
│ --output-format <ipynb|trx>           │ The output format to be used when    │
│                                       │ running a notebook with the --run    │
│                                       │ and --exit-after-run options         │
│ --output-path <--OUTPUT-PATH>         │ Run the file specified by --run and  │
│                                       │ writes the output to the file        │
│                                       │ specified by --output-path           │
│ --input <--INPUT>                     │ Specifies in a value for @input      │
│                                       │ tokens in magic commands in the      │
│                                       │ notebook, using the format --input   │
│                                       │ <key>=<value>. Values containing     │
│                                       │ spaces should be wrapped in quotes.  │
└───────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┘
🟢 Once it's running, here are some things you can do:
┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃ ⌨ Shortcut keys:                                                             ┃
┃ ┌─────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ┃
┃ │ Key         │ What it does                                               │ ┃
┃ ├─────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ ┃
┃ │ Shift+Enter │ Inserts a newline without submitting the current code      │ ┃
┃ │ Tab         │ Show next completion                                       │ ┃
┃ │ Shift+Tab   │ Show previous completion                                   │ ┃
┃ │ Ctrl+C      │ Exit the REPL                                              │ ┃
┃ │ Ctrl+Up     │ Go back through your submission history (current session   │ ┃
┃ │             │ only)                                                      │ ┃
┃ │ Ctrl+Down   │ Go forward through your submission history (current        │ ┃
┃ │             │ session only)                                              │ ┃
┃ └─────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ┃
┗━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┛

options.name: --log-path
options.description:
Enable file logging to the specified
directory

options.name: --default-kernel
options.description: The default language for the kernel
